US Patent 4405829 filed by Rivest, Shamir, and Adleman (https://patents.google.com/patent/US4405829A/en)

pi_items.metadata_value: A History of the ARPANET: The First Decade
pi_items.verified_value: A History of the ARPANET: The First Decade

pi_items.source: Turing's paper reprinted in 'The World of Mathematics' Vol. 4 (1956), edited by James R. Newman, pages 2099-2123 (confirmed via https://archive.org/stream/jamesr.newmantheworldofmathematicsvolume4simonandschusternewyork1956/)

privileged_info_summary: All four bibliographic items referenced in the task metadata are real historical works with verified titles. (1) John Forbes Nash Jr.'s 1950 PhD dissertation at Princeton University is titled 'Non-Cooperative Games'. (2) US Patent 4405829, issued to MIT in 1983 for the RSA cryptosystem, is titled 'Cryptographic communications system and method'. (3) Bolt Beranek and Newman's 1981 report on the ARPANET's first decade (Report No. 4799) is titled 'A History of the ARPANET: The First Decade'. (4) Alan Turing's seminal 1950 paper 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' was reprinted in James Newman's anthology 'The World of Mathematics' (Volume 4, 1956) at pages 2099-2123.
